stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Reopening the waterway is a sticking point in negotiations as oil supply bottlenecks fuel a global energy crisis

stories.headline: Trump Says US Will Blockade Hormuz Strait After Iran Talks Fail
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US would blockade the Strait of Hormuz following the collapse of peace talks with Iran in Islamabad. Axios National Security Editor Dave Lawler, Bloomberg News White House Reporter Jeff Mason and Congressional Reporter Alicia Diaz join Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
stories.summary: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption

stories.headline: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.headline: Trump threatens US 'blockade' of Strait of Hormuz after Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan fail
stories.summary: Trump said in a social media post he has “instructed our Navy to seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran. No one who pays an illegal toll will have safe passage on the high seas”.

stories.headline: US stock futures slide ahead of Iran ports blockade
stories.summary: Oil prices expected to rise as Trump tells US navy to ‘interdict’ ships that pay Iran to pass through strategic chokepoint

stories.headline: European Gas Prices Jump as Trump Threatens Hormuz Blockade
stories.summary: European natural gas prices surged in early Asia trading on Monday, after President Donald Trump said the US will begin a full blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Hormuz Tracker: Limited Transits Before Trump Announces Blockade
stories.summary: Shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz continued to operate at reduced levels on Sunday, before President Donald Trump announced an immediate blockade of the critical maritime chokepoint by the US Navy.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': BBC reports from Hungary as Viktor Orbán concedes election
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Oil prices soar above $100 per barrel as hope fades of end to Iran war
stories.summary: Crude had stabilised on optimism that a deal could be reached to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Peter Magyar’s Tisza wins Hungary election as Viktor Orban concedes
stories.summary: Centre-right party wins two-thirds majority in parliament that would allow it to amend constitution.

stories.headline: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.headline: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
stories.summary: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

stories.headline: Britain Faces Another Lost Year for Living Standards Due to Iran War
stories.summary: A typical UK household will be left nearly £500 ($672) worse off by surging energy prices, a setback for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s pledge to boost living standards.

stories.headline: Oil Surges With Gas as US Blockade of Hormuz Escalates Crisis
stories.summary: Oil and natural gas surged as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after weekend talks between Washington and Tehran failed to yield a deal, escalating a global energy crisis that’s rocked markets.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

stories.headline: Peter Magyar says his election win has ‘liberated Hungary’ from Orban
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has won Hungary’s election, defeating longtime nationalist leader Viktor Orban.

stories.headline: A2 Milk Lowers Guidance as Formula Supply Disrupted in China
stories.summary: New Zealand infant formula supplier a2 Milk lowered its revenue and earnings guidance, citing supply chain disruptions — partly linked to the Iran war — that have slowed shipments to China.

stories.headline: Calls grow for Rep. Swalwell to resign or be expelled over misconduct claims
stories.summary: Several Democratic and Republican lawmakers said they would be in favor of voting to expel Swalwell, as well as Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas), who both face misconduct allegations.

stories.headline: Abuse allegations against lead Democrat shake race for California governor
stories.summary: Media reports detailing sexual assault allegations against US Representative Eric Swalwell prompt resignation calls.

stories.headline: Malnourished Afghan minors trapped as two wars cut off supplies
Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files

stories.headline: China’s Victory Giant Seeks Up to $2.2 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
stories.summary: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. began taking investor orders for its second listing in Hong Kong, which may raise as much as HK$17.5 billion ($2.2 billion) in what is set to be among the city’s largest first-time share sales this year.

stories.headline: KKR Unit to Boost Buying in $2.8 Trillion Japan Property Market
stories.summary: KKR & Co.’s Japan real estate management subsidiary plans a “big expansion” in purchases of properties that companies want to sell off, a market the firm estimates is as big as ¥450 trillion ($2.8 trillion), the unit’s   head said.

stories.headline: GFL Said to Near Deal to Buy Secure Waste for Over $4.3 Billion
stories.summary: GFL Environmental Inc. is closing in on a deal to acquire Canada’s Secure Waste Infrastructure Corp. in a transaction valued at more than C$6 billion ($4.3 billion) including debt, according to a person familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Retail-trading Apps Go Upscale to Keep Rich Traders Loyal
stories.summary: Robinhood, eToro and Public are racing to keep their young clientele from “graduating” to Wall Street wealth managers with gala events, exclusive concierges, and platinum cards. Charlie Wells has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Fires Board That Oversees Presidio in San Francisco
stories.summary: All six board members that oversee the national historic landmark were terminated, part of the administration’s efforts to reshape the federal bureaucracy.

stories.summary: Top EU countries and US states appear one after the other in economic size rankings. However, US states surpass the top EU economies in GDP per capita, both in nominal terms and purchasing power.

stories.headline: Vance, who wasn’t keen on Iran war, now tasked with trying to end it

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
stories.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing

stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.
